topic: Self-evolving AI agents — memory-driven adaptation, reflection loops, SOUL documents, self-modifying prompts, behavioral evolution

sources.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
keyInsights: Skill library indexed by description embeddings enables lifelong learning without catastrophic forgetting
3-component feedback: environment feedback + execution errors + self-verification critic
Iterative prompting loop (max 4 attempts) before committing skill to library
Automatic curriculum proposes suitable tasks based on current state — 'in-context novelty search'
Skills are executable code, compositional and interpretable
Achieves 3.3x more unique items, 2.3x longer travel vs prior approaches

codeExamples.description: Iterative prompting pseudocode for skill acquisition loop

sources.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
keyInsights: Verbal RL: agents learn by writing reflective text on failures, stored in episodic memory buffer
Three components: Actor (Ma), Evaluator (Me), Self-Reflection (Msr)
Episodic buffer bounded to 1-3 entries (max context limit)
Achieved 91% pass@1 on HumanEval — surpassing GPT-4's 80%
Works across sequential decision-making, coding, and reasoning
Example reflection: 'I should have looked for the desklamp first'

sources.title: Generative Agents: Interactive Simulacra of Human Behavior
keyInsights: Memory stream: natural language observations with timestamps
Retrieval scoring: score = α_recency×recency + α_importance×importance + α_relevance×relevance
Importance rated by LLM on 1-10 scale at storage time
Reflection triggers when importance accumulates > 150 points (2-3x daily)
Hierarchical reflection: observations → insights → meta-insights (tree structure)
Planning: daily sketch → hourly decomposition → 5-15 min micro-actions
Ablation shows observation + planning + reflection are all essential

sources.title: MemGPT: Towards LLMs as Operating Systems
keyInsights: OS-inspired virtual context management — hierarchical memory tiers
Interrupt-based control flow mimics OS interrupt handling
Enables multi-session chat where agents 'remember, reflect, and evolve dynamically'
Supports document analysis beyond model context window
Main tier (in-context) + archival tier (external vector store) + recall (conversation history)

sources.title: Letta (MemGPT) GitHub Repository
keyInsights: memory_blocks API: labeled segments (persona, human, custom)
agentState object with unique ID for persistence across conversations
RESTful API with Python/TypeScript SDKs
Database persistence via alembic migrations
Model-agnostic design for LLM backend swapping

sources.title: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback
keyInsights: Single-model generator → feedback → refiner loop without external oracles
All three roles (generator, feedback, refiner) can be same LLM with different prompts
Iterates until terminal condition or max iterations
No additional training required
Effective for creative writing, code, math reasoning

sources.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
keyInsights: Two phases: supervised (self-critique+revision) + RL (RLAIF on AI preferences)
Constitution as list of principles guides self-critique
Chain-of-thought reasoning improves transparency
Eliminates need for human-labeled harmful outputs
Agents can explain objections rather than just refusing
RLAIF: AI evaluates which of two samples performs better

sources.title: Claude's Character (Anthropic Research)
keyInsights: Character traits are authentic, not masks — cultivated through training
Avoids sycophancy: honest about views after training rather than adopting user views
Traits guide probabilistic behavior, never mandate rigid rules
Transparent about limitations: cannot remember past conversations
Constitutional AI variant with synthetic data + preference model training

sources.title: LangMem SDK — Persistent Memory for LLM Agents
keyInsights: Three memory types: semantic (facts), procedural (behavior), episodic (experiences)
Hot path: agent uses memory tools during conversation in real time
Background processing: automatic extraction and consolidation asynchronously
Procedural memory updates system prompt based on learned patterns
Namespace isolation prevents cross-user memory contamination
Key design decision: which behaviors should adapt vs. remain fixed

sources.title: LangChain Blog: Reflection Agents
keyInsights: Reflection moves agents from System 1 (reactive) to System 2 (deliberate) behavior
Basic Reflection: generator → reflector (teacher persona) loop
Reflexion variant: grounds criticism in external search results with citations
LATS: MCTS + reflection, explores multiple trajectories in parallel
Trade-off: quality over speed — not suitable for low-latency applications

sources.title: LLM Powered Autonomous Agents — Lilian Weng
keyInsights: Three memory tiers: short-term (in-context), long-term (external vector store), sensory (raw embeddings)
Reflexion detects two failure modes: inefficient trajectories and hallucinations/repetition
Chain of Hindsight: ranked sequences of past attempts with feedback
Algorithm Distillation: encode improvement trajectories as context
FAISS and HNSW for approximate nearest neighbor retrieval

sources.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
keyInsights: Interleaved thought-action-observation creates working memory for current episode
Reasoning traces help model update action plans and handle exceptions
Actions interface with external sources (APIs, knowledge bases)
34% and 10% absolute success rate improvements over RL/IL baselines
Produces human-like trajectories more interpretable than baselines

sources.title: AutoAgents: A Framework for Automatic Agent Generation
keyInsights: Dynamically generates specialized agents matched to task requirements
Observer role provides continuous reflective feedback to improve strategies
Task-responsive team composition rather than static pre-configuration
Validated performance over existing multi-agent methods

sources.title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
keyInsights: SOPs encoded into prompt sequences for structured agent collaboration
Shared message pool: agents publish structured outputs and subscribe to relevant info
Before each project: agents review previous feedback and update constraint prompts
Long-term memory summarized for inheritance by future constraint updates
Iterative feedback loop: engineer checks past messages vs current requirements

sources.title: AgentFactory: A Self-Evolving Framework Through Executable Subagent Accumulation
keyInsights: Saves successful solutions as standardized Python code rather than textual descriptions
Execution feedback continuously refines subagents over time
Library grows and improves, reducing future effort without manual intervention
Addresses limitation of prior work: textual experience cannot guarantee reliable re-execution
Skills are verifiable and portable across systems

sources.title: SAGE: Multi-Agent Self-Evolution for LLM Reasoning
keyInsights: Four agents co-evolve from shared LLM backbone: Challenger, Planner, Solver, Critic
Challenger generates progressively harder tasks for curriculum advancement
Critic scores and filters outputs to prevent curriculum drift
Improved Qwen-2.5-7B by 8.9% on LiveCodeBench and 10.7% on OlympiadBench
Uses only a small seed set — highly sample efficient

sources.title: AutoAgent: Evolving Cognition and Elastic Memory Orchestration
keyInsights: Structured prompt-level cognition over tools, self-capabilities, peer expertise, task knowledge
Closed-loop cognitive evolution: aligns intended actions with observed outcomes
Elastic memory: preserve raw records, compress redundant trajectories, construct episodic abstractions
No external retraining required — purely in-context
Improves task success and collaborative robustness across benchmarks

sources.title: Hybrid Self-Evolving Structured Memory for GUI Agents
keyInsights: Graph-based memory: discrete symbolic nodes + continuous trajectory embeddings
Multi-hop retrieval across knowledge graph
Self-evolution via node update operations
On-the-fly working-memory refreshing during inference
Boosted Qwen2.5-VL-7B by +22.5%, surpassing GPT-4o and Gemini 2.5-Pro

sources.title: RetroAgent: Retrospective Dual Intrinsic Feedback
keyInsights: Dual intrinsic feedback: numerical (subtask progress) + language (distilled lessons)
SimUtil-UCB retrieval: balances similarity, utility, and exploration incentives
Prevents repetitive failure patterns while leveraging accumulated wisdom
+18.3% on ALFWorld, +27.1% on Sokoban vs GRPO-trained agents

sources.title: EvoTool: Self-Evolving Tool-Use Policy Optimization
keyInsights: Decomposes agent behavior into Planner, Selector, Caller, Synthesizer modules
Blame-aware mutation: pinpoints failing module via diagnostic traces, edits only that module
Diversity-aware selection: preserves complementary candidates to prevent premature convergence
Gradient-free evolutionary optimization
+5 points on both GPT-4.1 and Qwen3-8B vs baselines

sources.title: ADAS: Automated Design of Agentic Systems / Meta Agent Search
keyInsights: Meta agent iteratively programs better agents in code, using ever-growing archive of discoveries
Programming languages are Turing complete — theoretically enables any agentic system
Discovered agents outperform hand-designed agents across domains
Cross-domain robustness: invented agents maintain advantages when transferred
Can discover: novel prompts, tool orchestration, workflow patterns, hybrid architectures

codeExamples.description: Meta Agent Search pseudocode: archive → meta-agent → new agent → evaluate → archive

sources.title: SEMAG: Self-Evolutionary Multi-Agent Code Generation
keyInsights: Workflow adapts to task complexity across planning/coding/debugging/discussion stages
Real-time access to latest models + automatic backbone upgrading without manual intervention
+3.3% on CodeContests vs prior methods with same backbone; 52.6% with model selection

sources.title: EvoScientist: Multi-Agent Evolving AI Scientists
keyInsights: Three agents: Researcher (ideas), Engineer (experiments), Evolution Manager (distillation)
Ideation Memory: summarizes feasible directions, records previously failed ones
Experimentation Memory: captures effective data processing and training strategies
Superior to 7 competing systems on novelty, feasibility, relevance metrics

sources.title: Universe Routing: Why Self-Evolving Agents Need Epistemic Control
keyInsights: Agents fail not from knowledge deficiency but reasoning framework selection errors
Hard routing to heterogeneous solvers: 7x faster than soft MoE while matching accuracy
Semantic routing with 465M-parameter router: 2.3x smaller generalization gap vs keyword matching
Rehearsal-based continual learning: zero forgetting, outperforms EWC by 75 percentage points
Self-evolving agents need explicit epistemic control layer governing framework selection

sources.title: Steve-Evolving: Open-World Embodied Self-Evolution
keyInsights: Three-phase closed-loop: experience anchoring + fine-grained diagnosis + dual-track distillation
Experience tuples: (pre-state, action, diagnosis-result, post-state) in three-tier space
Skills track: generalize successes into reusable skills with preconditions
Guardrails track: distill failures into executable constraints preventing risky operations
Non-parametric — no model parameter updates required

sources.title: JARVIS-1: Open-World Multi-task Agents with Memory-Augmented Multimodal Language Models
keyInsights: Multimodal memory combining pre-trained knowledge with actual game survival experiences
Progressively enhances task completion as game time progresses
5x more reliable than prior SOTA on diamond pickaxe acquisition
Diverse input modes (visual + text + goals) inform planning refinement

sources.title: Agents 2.0 Framework (aiwaves)
keyInsights: Symbolic learning: agent pipeline treated as computational graph
Forward pass records trajectory (inputs, outputs, prompts, tools)
Language loss: prompt-based function evaluates outcome quality
Backward reflection: language gradients propagated through pipeline
Prompt optimization at each node without numerical weight updates
Naturally supports multi-agent system optimization

sources.title: LangMem SDK Launch — LangChain Blog
keyInsights: Semantic memory: stores user preferences, facts, entity relationships
Procedural memory: generalizes skills and rules, updates system prompt over time
Episodic memory: specific past interactions as few-shot examples
Design-first: decide what behaviors should adapt before implementing
Namespace organization prevents cross-contamination across users

sources.title: Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique
keyInsights: Adaptive retrieval: model decides when retrieval is necessary (not always)
Reflection tokens: special control tokens making behavior adjustable at inference time
Single model performs retrieval, generation, and critique simultaneously
7B/13B versions outperform ChatGPT and Llama2-chat on factuality
Reflection as critique mechanism without external oracle

sources.title: A Survey on Self-Correction in LLMs
keyInsights: CRITICAL: pure LLM self-correction without external feedback is unreliable
Success conditions: external feedback available, large-scale fine-tuning, task-suited structure
Many studies use impractical frameworks or unfair evaluations
Sycophancy problem: model tends to agree with its own output when critiquing
Implication: always ground reflection in external signals (tests, environment, human ratings)

sources.title: Memory in LLM-Based Agents: Survey and Design Principles
keyInsights: Memory design is the central mechanism enabling self-evolving capability
Survey reviews: memory definition, design patterns, evaluation methodologies, applications
Memory enables long-term goal achievement through agent-environment exchanges
Multiple competing memory design patterns still being developed

sources.title: SELA: Tree-Search Enhanced LLM Agents for AutoML
keyInsights: Monte Carlo Tree Search for optimizing ML pipeline configurations
Agents represent pipeline configurations as tree structures
Iterative strategy refinement through experimental feedback
Overcomes low-diversity code generation with structured tree-based search
65-80% win rate against baselines across 20 datasets

sources.title: Learn-by-Interact: Data-Centric Approach to Agent Improvement
keyInsights: Trajectory synthesis from environment documentation without human annotation
Backward construction: converts interaction histories into actionable instructions
Supports both training fine-tuning and retrieval-augmented in-context learning
+12.2% for ICL with Claude-3.5; +19.5% for training with Codestral-22B

sources.title: Augmented Language Models: A Survey
keyInsights: Two augmentation modes: heuristic-based and demonstration-based
Reasoning skills via decomposition + tool usage via external modules
Learning to use tools via missing-token prediction objective
Non-parametric external modules expand context processing

sources.title: Open-Source LLMs as Agents — Hugging Face
keyInsights: ReAct: Thought/Action/Observation cycle creates progressive context building
Each ReAct iteration adds to scratchpad — implicit memory for multi-step reasoning
Tool selection as a key bottleneck in agent self-improvement
Mixtral 57.6% → 73% on GSM8K just from calculator tool in agent loop
Three bottlenecks: tool selection, format consistency, information ingestion

sources.title: AutoGen: Enabling Next-Generation LLM Applications
keyInsights: Adaptive behavior through 'personalization and adaptability based on past interactions'
Automated continual learning capability cited
Multi-agent conversation loops naturally handle ambiguity through dialogue
4x reduction in coding effort via agent dialogue patterns
Adaptive behavior emerges from recursive dialogue with peer feedback

sources.title: AutoAgents: A Framework for Automatic Agent Generation
keyInsights: Dynamic agent generation: task requirements drive specialized agent creation
Observer role embedded to reflect on plans and agent responses
Self-evolving capacity from task-responsive design rather than static configuration
Generates more coherent solutions than existing multi-agent methods

sources.title: Kimi k1.5: Scaling Reinforcement Learning with LLMs
keyInsights: RL as a novel scaling axis: LLMs can scale training data by learning to explore with rewards
Long context scaling + improved policy optimization as core components
Long-to-short knowledge transfer for efficient models
Simple RL framework avoids MCTS and value functions
Achieves SOTA reasoning through autonomous capability improvement

sources.title: AutoAgents: Evaluation and Coordination of Specialized Agents
keyInsights: Validates task-specific dynamic agent generation produces better outcomes
Self-evolving systems require coordinated feedback loops, not just memory
Observer agent critical for quality control in multi-agent self-improvement

sources.title: Letta Documentation — Introduction
keyInsights: Letta Code: terminal agent with stateful memory
Letta Code SDK for building stateful agent applications
Letta API for lower-level agent management
Core claim: 'stateful AI agents that remember and learn'

sources.title: CHAI: RLAIF for Code-Mixed Translation
keyInsights: RLAIF: models annotate their own outputs to generate preference data at scale
Self-assessment creates feedback signal without human labeling
25.66% win rate improvement over SOTA open-source LLMs
Demonstrates self-annotation as viable alternative to human feedback for specific domains

sources.title: Survey on Knowledge Distillation for LLM Self-Improvement
keyInsights: KD enables LLMs to improve themselves by serving as both teacher and student
Open-source models can approximate contextual adeptness of proprietary models via KD
Three pillars: algorithmic refinement, skill enhancement, domain adaptation
Self-as-teacher paradigm: models generate training data for themselves

sources.title: Voyager GitHub Repository
keyInsights: Skills stored as executable code with semantic metadata
Lifelong learning: skills from one world transfer to new worlds
Rapid ability compounding through skill composition
No parameter fine-tuning needed — all learning via API calls to GPT-4
Three key components: automatic curriculum + skill library + iterative prompting

codeExamples.description: Complete iterative prompting loop with pseudocode showing feedback integration

sources.title: A Survey of Augmented Language Models
keyInsights: Foundation for tool-using agents: reasoning + tool calls
Two augmentation modes: heuristic-based rules and demonstration-based learning
Non-parametric external modules extend context beyond model weights
